item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I remember first looking through your draft and telling you guys that the problem with the final addendum was that the language was impenetrable - in its original form, the story was so vague that you couldn't even agree on what was happening.

I feel like you went overboard in trying to fix it: now, you reveal too much in too plain of a way. I'm echoing the sentiment here, but I can understand why you wrote the final product this way. You took out a lot of the dramatic language I liked as well - for instance, I thought it was a nice flair to end it with "Don't fight fire with fire - or the whole world will burn." except you cut the last part off in the final draft.

My tip is to keep some of the symbolism and the between-the-lines sensibilities while keeping it from being meaningless and vague. There's a fine balance between the two, and I hope to see that in your future writing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I saw this go through drafts, and I said the narrative could be expanded upon, but not in this way. My dislike of this article is in the final note, as with many other readers: it doesn't leave me wanting more information, or alternatively there isn't anything to analyze. When everything is so straightforward, it makes the SCP seem bland and unoriginal. Any concept, even an unoriginal one can be done with good writing.

This is an example of an "ok" idea done with "ok" writing. I didn't think it was mainlist-ready yet, but it's a step up and decent writing. However my afterthoughts when reading made me novote this, because of its revealing of too much information.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love this, and I love SCPDeclassified (I've made requests that got filled!!!!!)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As others said, the most problematic part of this SCP is the final letter, it's just too convenient of a "here's the answer to all your questions".

Also doesn't help that this is "set" like a hundred years ago, right? Shouldn't the word-choice of a letter from that time reflect this?

Some of the content of the letter is worth keeping I think, but I'd adjust the framing device and drastically reduce the detail.

Still, the rest is quite good and I like the GoI. Upvoted, but with reservations.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Twenty four votes and its at zero?

I'm impressed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I applaud the efforts behind any collaboration, but I cannot +1 this. The ever generic "something isn't what is seems oooooooo" and "documentation what hints at a greater narrative but will likely never be expanded upon" do nothing to enhance or even distract from the bland concept of the anomaly. There are also formatting errors in the interview logs and its/it's confusion. -1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Whilst I can appreciate the clear effort and care put behind this article, the execution suffers from a severe lack of subtlety. Nothing here really interests or engages me, and as others have said, the ending note is a jarring kind of expository. Perhaps it's just not my kind of thing.

The best articles are the ones you remember a day later. This, unfortunately, seemed to fall out of my head as soon as I read the last word. -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I enjoyed the interviews (though, I'm certain it could have been just as illustrative with less entries), for what it's worth, but I feel like the endnote is far too expository - I would have been content with something much less revealing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Seconding strongly, I really feel the final note just breaks this article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ show block
– hide block

thaumiel is a mistake httfbttwnypd

Bt what about "httfbttwnypd"?

Anyway, for all its talk on an erased past involving various anomalies and a precursor, I was reminded of my own 001 proposal.

The contrast between the information bits acquired from the interviews versus the final note is quite jarring. Of course, that contrast would be due to the speakers (i.e. traumatised victims versus the researcher).

We, sadly, don't really get information regarding the green suits' ideology, beyond what that researcher has to say (who does not seem to be fully in favour of his own organisation). It would help if it is more fleshed out from a counterpart to GOC or some 'destroy anomalies' group.

On another note, using one photograph as the sole amnestic for an entire organisation would have been very troublesome.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
An scp that serves as a record of an early foundation's crimes before focusing on containment is a great concept, and I quite enjoy the memory logs. What sinks this is the final note, which is far too convenient, over explanatory, far too long, and ultimately unsatisfying.

A better ending could have been an additional memory log in which it is heavily implied that the soldiers are early Foundation, possibly including the taking of the picture at the site of an anomaly's death. Showing rather than telling. I wish you guys luck in improving the piece.

item.title: Plot Spoilers
item.content:encoded:
Since it was I who was responsible for the second addendum, along with that long-ass final letter, I feel I owe you an apology explanation.

+ show block
– hide block

So, the greenshirts are a GoI. It’s never explicitly stated whether they’re a precursor to the Foundation, the Foundation from an alternate universe, or just an unrelated faction. However, there is a small hint that implies that they’re a Foundation precursor:

only thirteen of us left

I just threw that in because it’s my headcanon. Anyhow, the GoI began by just searching for anomalies, but soon decides to use the SCPs to weaponize themselves to fight the more dangerous ones (Keter). The bloke writing the note is vehemently against this, but he gets overruled.

The culmination of this is the photograph. Since the GoI is tracking and snatching anomalies from the shadows (another nod to the GoI being a Foundation precursor), they need to make people forget. And how should they do this, but with an anomaly?

They task the science guy with creating something to help them forget. And lo and behold, the photo is created. Thing is, it becomes slightly too good, and starts erasing the memories of random people. So then they need the scientist to reverse its effects. The trouble with that is that he can't remember how he created the photo.

Suddenly, he realizes that, since it's a photograph, there must have been a negative of that photograph which would have the opposite effect. Sure enough, it exists, and it's indestructible.

However, the problem with the Negative is that it doesn't restore memories - it gives people random memories it had erased. Memories of anomalies, as experienced by people who didn't know what they were. Hence, the D-Class testing logs in the first addendum recalling random memories of anomalies.

This caused serious fractures within the GoI, and by the time they stopped using it there were only thirteen left. Through mysterious circumstances, twelve of the thirteen died, leaving only the scientist left with both photographs. The scientist writes this note, stares at it for a long, long time to increase the chances of it being remembered, then glances at the positive, and forgets everything.

Where does this leave us? The article is about the negative; it restores the wrong memories, but since it happened so long ago there were no memories to overwrite in the D-Class. This means that somewhere out there is a photograph able to completely erase anything from your memory. Who knows… maybe someone has it. Maybe someone is using it to remain completely invisible, from everyone including the Foundation.

Also, quick secret: read the first letter of every paragraph for a hidden warning by the author.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
Hello! This is collaboration SCP written by YossiLeiner, BlazingTrail and I. We all come from the small SCP Declassified reddit. Normally we explain SCPs, but we decided to write one for a change!

Special thanks to the following users for their critique on the forums and IRC chat. We wouldn't have been able to get here without their help.

  • Modulum
  • CaptainGirth
  • Benjamin Stokes
  • Alfred Hitchcock
  • Zachary Maxwell
  • taylor_itkin
  • Icepick
+ SCPDeclassified Discord Chatlogs 07/17/2017 EST Time (Minor Spoilers)
- Concepts addressed here may or may not represent the final product

(12:47 PM) Yossi: I have an idea for a SCiP
(12:47 PM) RockDHouse🎹: Hit it up
(12:49 PM) Yossi: It's a memetic which gives random memories to those who look at it. While considered a regular anomaly at first, it was later discovered it was also pumping out humes, which led to the discovery that it was an antimemetic on another dimension, which took people's memeories, and also took in humes
(12:50 PM) RockDHouse🎹: I like the idea but I question the necessity of the humes
(12:50 PM) Yossi: Well, how else would they discovery it's other properties?
(12:50 PM) RockDHouse🎹: A pattern maybe
(12:50 PM) RockDHouse🎹: They find memories that compliment each other
(12:51 PM) RockDHouse🎹: Tell a story
(12:51 PM) Yossi: Maybe not in different dimensions, but two objects in the same dimension?
(12:51 PM) RockDHouse🎹: And they find the compliment?
(12:52 PM) Yossi: When someone recieves the memories, they track the owner of the memories. With a pattern, they pinpoint the location of the second object
(12:52 PM) RockDHouse🎹: It's a concept
(12:53 PM) RockDHouse🎹: But I'd like to find a story in there
(12:53 PM) Yossi: hmmm
(12:53 PM) Yossi: like what?
(12:54 PM) RockDHouse🎹: Maybe it's just one object
(12:54 PM) RockDHouse🎹: And it's been around for a while
(12:55 PM) RockDHouse🎹: And some precursor GOI- an evil one -is using it as an anmestic
(12:55 PM) RockDHouse🎹: Wiping out memories of their crimes
(12:55 PM) RockDHouse🎹: And so it gets lost for a while
(12:56 PM) RockDHouse🎹: The Foundation picks it up and now all it does is release these memories
(12:56 PM) RockDHouse🎹: And so over the course of the document we learn about the old GOI or something
(12:56 PM) RockDHouse🎹: The things they do
(12:56 PM) RockDHouse🎹: I dunno if that's made it more generic but it's an idea
(12:57 PM) Yossi: Seems like a good idea actually
(12:57 PM) Yossi: Thanks man
(12:58 PM) RockDHouse🎹: Oh, I just thought of a twist
(12:58 PM) Yossi: Yea?
(12:59 PM) RockDHouse🎹: It turns out the GOI is actually a precursor of the Foundation
(12:59 PM) RockDHouse🎹: And that everything about it was wiped out in one of the many resets done to the Foundation's universe
(12:59 PM) Yossi: OH
(12:59 PM) Yossi: OH SHOOT THAT'S GOOD
(1:00 PM) Yossi: Like, I version of the Foundation, before amnestics were discovered.
(1:00 PM) RockDHouse🎹: Yeah, kinda
(1:02 PM) RockDHouse🎹: I was thinking that would be the note to end on
(1:02 PM) Yossi: Yea
(1:02 PM) Yossi: I was thinking that too
